topStories.headline: Hawks Stun Knicks, Wolves Erase 19-Point Hole — Game 2s Deliver Chaos
body: CJ McCollum poured in 32 points as the Atlanta Hawks rallied with a 28-15 fourth quarter to steal Game 2 at Madison Square Garden and even their first-round series 1-1. In Denver, the Minnesota Timberwolves authored the night's most stunning comeback — erasing a 19-point deficit to beat the Nuggets 119-114 and tie that series as well. Donovan Mitchell was clinical for Cleveland, posting 30 points, 7 rebounds and 5 assists as the Cavaliers took a 2-0 series lead over Toronto. The Los Angeles Lakers also opened strong, winning Game 1 over Houston 107-98 with Luke Kennard setting a career playoff high of 27 points and LeBron James adding 19 points and 13 assists against a Rockets team missing Kevin Durant.

topStories.headline: Fitzpatrick Sinks Playoff Birdie to Deny World No. 1 Scheffler at Harbour Town
body: Matt Fitzpatrick holed a 12-foot birdie putt on the first extra hole Sunday to claim the RBC Heritage title in a playoff over world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler at Harbour Town Golf Links. Fitzpatrick earned $3.6 million from the $20 million purse in a result that gives him real momentum heading into the major season. Si Woo Kim finished third. Scheffler, who had held the 54-hole lead, was denied by Fitzpatrick's poise under pressure — a rare blemish on an otherwise exceptional 2026 for the world number one.

topStories.headline: Flyers Silence Pittsburgh Twice, Hurricanes Survive Double-OT as East Heats Up
body: The Philadelphia Flyers shut out the Pittsburgh Penguins 3-0 to grab a commanding 2-0 series lead in the Battle of Pennsylvania — a dominant start that has Sidney Crosby and the Penguins facing an early elimination threat. The Carolina Hurricanes survived double overtime, outlasting the Ottawa Senators 3-2 to lead their series 2-0. Connor McDavid and the defending-champion Edmonton Oilers opened their title defense with a gritty 4-3 win over the Anaheim Ducks. Upsets are brewing: the Montreal Canadiens lead Tampa Bay 1-0 and the Buffalo Sabres lead Boston 1-0 after Game 1 victories.

topStories.headline: Reddick Makes History With Fifth Win in Nine Races at Kansas
body: Tyler Reddick overtook Kyle Larson on the final lap of overtime at Kansas Speedway to win the AdventHealth 400 — his fifth Cup Series victory in the first nine races of 2026. Only four drivers in NASCAR history have ever won five of the first nine races in a season. Reddick survived a fuel strategy scramble and a late caution before sealing the win by 0.118 seconds. Chase Briscoe (3rd), Denny Hamlin (4th), and Bubba Wallace (5th) rounded out the top five. The Cup Series heads to Dover Motor Speedway next weekend.

stories.headline: Wolves Come Back From 19, Hawks Steal MSG — Cavs Roll to 2-0 Series Lead
body: Minnesota's 19-point second-half comeback against the Nuggets was the evening's signature moment — the Timberwolves locked in defensively in the fourth quarter, holding Denver to just 20 points in the final frame. In New York, CJ McCollum's 32-point explosion powered the Hawks to a 107-106 road win, Atlanta outscoring the Knicks 28-15 in the final frame. Cleveland looks like the most complete team in the East through two games, with Donovan Mitchell leading every key moment. The Oklahoma City Thunder sent an early statement in the West — their 119-84 Game 1 demolition of Phoenix signals that the defending champions are on a mission.

stories.headline: Embiid's Appendectomy Clouds Celtics-Sixers, Durant Sits for Houston
body: Joel Embiid is a major question mark after his emergency appendectomy on April 9. The average NBA recovery window of 23 days puts his first-round availability against Boston in serious doubt — an enormous blow for Philadelphia, already the underdog against the Celtics. The Sixers received a slightly positive update ahead of Game 2 but nothing definitive. The Houston Rockets were also without Kevin Durant in their Game 1 loss to the Lakers — without KD's scoring load, Houston's offense was blunted against the experienced Los Angeles defense.

stories.headline: Flyers and Hurricanes Jump to 2-0 Leads; Oilers Launch Title Defense
body: The Philadelphia Flyers are two wins from a stunning first-round sweep of the Pittsburgh Penguins — winning both games at home in the Battle of Pennsylvania, including a shutout in Game 2. The Carolina Hurricanes, the East's top seed, survived a double-overtime scare against Ottawa to also lead 2-0. In the West, defending-champion Edmonton opened with a 4-3 win over Anaheim — Connor McDavid and company showing no championship hangover. The Dallas Stars bounced back with a 4-2 Game 2 win to even their series with Minnesota at 1-1.

stories.headline: East Upsets Brewing: Sabres Lead Bruins, Canadiens Lead Lightning 1-0
body: The East bracket took on a volatile look early. The Buffalo Sabres opened by surprising the Boston Bruins in Game 1, while an improved Montreal Canadiens team beat the Tampa Bay Lightning in their series opener. If either underdog extends their lead to 2-0 this week, the Eastern Conference picture would be dramatically reshaped heading into the second round. Both Tampa and Boston face real pressure to respond emphatically in their upcoming Game 2s.

stories.headline: Red Sox Edge Tigers 8-6 as Early-Season MLB Races Take Shape
body: The Boston Red Sox held off the Detroit Tigers 8-6 on Sunday in a high-scoring affair, continuing their competitive early-season form. With the NBA and NHL playoffs commanding the weekend headlines, MLB hums along in the background: the Los Angeles Dodgers remain the NL's class act with Shohei Ohtani anchoring the lineup, while unexpected division leaders are beginning to emerge across both leagues. The season approaches its one-quarter mark this week with standings battles in every division still very much unsettled.

stories.headline: PSG vs. Bayern, Arsenal vs. Atlético — Europe's Final Four Set for April 28-29
body: The UEFA Champions League semifinals are locked in after a dramatic quarterfinal stage. PSG — who dismantled Liverpool 4-0 on aggregate — host Bayern Munich on April 28 at the Parc des Princes. Bayern had beaten Real Madrid in an extraordinary 6-4 aggregate tie, with Luis Díaz and Michael Olise sealing the comeback in Munich. On April 29, Atlético Madrid host Arsenal — Diego Simeone's defensive machine against Mikel Arteta's structured press. Atlético eliminated Barcelona 3-2 on aggregate; Arsenal edged through 1-0 against Sporting. Harry Kane is Bayern's danger man; Kai Havertz has been Arsenal's UCL clutch performer. Return legs: May 5-6. Final: Budapest, May 30.

stories.headline: No April Grand Prix: F1 Championship Battle on Hold Until Miami
body: Formula 1's 2026 season has an unplanned break in April after the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix were cancelled due to regional instability — replacement venues could not be organized on short notice. The championship resumes at the Miami Grand Prix (May 1-3). Through three rounds, teenager Kimi Antonelli leads the standings with 72 points after his Chinese GP win, with Mercedes teammate George Russell nine points back at 63. Charles Leclerc (49) keeps Ferrari in contention and Lewis Hamilton (41) remains a factor. The break gives teams extra development time ahead of an intense Americas swing.

stories.headline: Rybakina Wins Stuttgart, Madrid Open Begins as Clay Season Intensifies
body: Elena Rybakina defeated Karolína Muchová 7-5, 6-1 to win the Porsche Tennis Grand Prix in Stuttgart, adding to her growing clay-court credentials ahead of Roland Garros. The win reinforces Rybakina as a top contender on any surface heading into the swing's marquee events. The Mutua Madrid Open — a combined ATP Masters 1000 and WTA 1000 clay event — began Monday and runs through May 3, drawing the full complement of top players. Carlos Alcaraz, the two-time defending Madrid champion, headlines the ATP draw on his best surface as the clay season builds toward the French Open.

stories.headline: Fitzpatrick Drains Playoff Birdie to Edge World No. 1 Scheffler at Harbour Town
body: Matt Fitzpatrick claimed the 2026 RBC Heritage title in a playoff over world No. 1 Scottie Scheffler, sinking a clutch 12-foot birdie on the first extra hole at Harbour Town Golf Links. Fitzpatrick earned $3.6 million from the $20 million purse. Si Woo Kim took third. Scheffler — who had held the 54-hole lead with Webb Simpson sliding out of contention on Sunday — was denied by Fitzpatrick's poise under pressure, a rare playoff loss for the world number one. The win gives Fitzpatrick real momentum heading into the major season, with the PGA Championship at Quail Hollow the next big target.

stories.headline: Reddick Passes Larson on Final Lap for Historic 5th Cup Win in Nine Races
body: Tyler Reddick made a decisive last-lap move on Kyle Larson after an overtime restart at Kansas Speedway on Sunday, winning the AdventHealth 400 by 0.118 seconds for his fifth Cup Series victory in the first nine races of 2026. Reddick joins an elite group — only four drivers in NASCAR history have won five of the opening nine races in a season. A late caution by Cody Ware triggered the overtime that ultimately swung the race to Reddick after fuel strategy drama earlier in the event. Chase Briscoe (3rd), Denny Hamlin (4th), and Bubba Wallace (5th) completed the top five. The Cup Series heads to Dover Motor Speedway next weekend.
